normalized_definitions.material_designation_and_classification: The standard designation, trade name, commercial grade, UNS code, broad categorization, and specific alloy family classification used to identify and group the base material based on distinct chemical characteristics.
normalized_definitions.chemical_composition: The precise chemical elemental makeup of the alloy or base material, including trace elements, typically expressed in specific units of measure such as weight percent (wt%) or atomic percent (at%).
normalized_definitions.microstructural_properties: Details describing the internal structure of the material, including the overall microstructure, secondary phases, precipitates, intermetallic compounds, non-metallic inclusions, and the quantitative volume fractions of these phases that influence localized pitting resistance.
normalized_definitions.material_synthesis_and_purity: Information detailing the synthesis of the base alloy, including the melting methodology, deoxidation practices, and the specific purity levels of the starting elemental granules or master alloys that dictate trace impurity concentrations.
normalized_definitions.pitting_resistance_equivalent_number: The calculated Pitting Resistance Equivalent Number (PREN) or Pitting Index based on elemental composition (e.g., %Cr + 3(%Mo) + 10(%N)), used as a theoretical predictor for localized corrosion resistance.
normalized_definitions.crystallographic_properties: The identified crystallographic phases (e.g., FCC, BCC, B2, Sigma phase) present in the base material and their explicitly measured lattice parameters, which indicate structural stability and influence localized corrosion.
normalized_definitions.material_hardness: The measured mechanical hardness of the bulk alloy (e.g., Vickers hardness), which serves as an indicator of atomic disorder, phase distribution, and overall structural and electrochemical stability.
